title: Chains of large gaps between primes

thm:1.summary: $G_k(X) \gg (\log X \log_2 X \log_4 X) / (k^2 \log_3 X)$ — chains of $k$ consecutive large gaps. Settles a 1948 Erdős-Rankin question, generalizing both 1408.4505 and 1408.5110 from one gap to chains.

lem:1.1.summary: Chebyshev-type second-moment bound on $F(\mathbf{X},\mathbf{Y})$ for conditionally independent random variables. Generic probabilistic input for the random-covering construction.

lem:2.1.summary: Landau-Page theorem: at most one Siegel-Landau zero of $L(s,\chi)$ for primitive $\chi$ of modulus $\le Q$ in the standard zero-free region; if it exists, the exceptional modulus is square-free with $q_Q \gg \log^2 Q / \log_2^2 Q$.

cor:1.summary: Existence of $B_Q$ ($= 1$ or a prime $\gg \log_2 Q$) such that primes coprime to $B_Q$ satisfy uniform zero-free-region equidistribution. The 'Siegel modulus' the entire sieve has to dodge.

lem:2.2.summary: Gallagher's PNT in arithmetic progressions: under the zero-free hypothesis, primes $\equiv a\,(q)$ are at least $x/(\varphi(q)\log x)$ for $x \ge q^D$. The unconditional input on prime distribution.

thm:2.summary: Sieving an interval (deterministic core): for $y = c x \log x \log_3 x / \log_2 x$ and any prime $B_0 \in [\log x, x]$ (or $B_0 = 1$), one can choose residues $a_p \pmod{p}$ for primes $p \le x$, $p \ne B_0$, that produce a sparse sieved set. The Maynard 1408.5110 construction in the chains setting.

lem:3.1.summary: Existence of $B_0$ ($= 1$ or a prime in $[\log x, x]$) such that, with $P = P(x)/B_0$, the Linnik-style equidistribution $\#\{z \le Z : Pz + a \text{ prime}\} \gg (\log x / \log Z) Z$ holds for all coprime $a$ and $Z \ge P^D$. The Bombieri-Vinogradov-on-residues input feeding thm:2.

thm:3.summary: Sieving primes (random covering): randomly choose vectors $\vec{\mathbf{a}}, \vec{\mathbf{n}}$ so that with probability bounded below, the random union of residue classes covers all but a small fraction of the primes in $(x, y]$. The FGKT 1408.4505 random-covering theorem, generalized to chains.

thm:4.summary: Bipartite covering theorem: given $\mathcal{P}', \mathcal{Q}'$ sets of primes and random subsets $\mathbf{e}_p \subset \mathcal{Q}'$ of bounded size $r$ with sparsity bound $\mathbb{P}(q \in \mathbf{e}_p) \le x^{-1/2-1/10}$ and second-moment hypotheses, one can find a subset of $\mathbf{e}_p$ covering $\mathcal{Q}'$ minus $o(|\mathcal{Q}'|)$. The abstract probabilistic theorem distinguishing FMT from its predecessors.

thm:5.summary: Existence of admissible chain constructions: combines thm:2 and thm:3 to produce $k$ consecutive gaps of size $\gg G(X)$. The structural step that turns 'one large gap' into 'a chain of $k$'.

thm:6.summary: Quantitative version of the chain construction with explicit dependence on $k$. Proves the $k^2$ denominator in thm:1 is sharp up to constants.

lem:6.1.summary: Singular-series factorization for the chain construction: for primes-in-AP correlations across the $k$ chained intervals.

cor:2.summary: Specialization of thm:5 to $k = O(1)$ recovers the FGKT/Maynard single-gap bound.

lem:6.2.summary: Concentration of the chained covering count around its singular-series mean.

lem:6.3.summary: Independence-style estimate for chained $\Lambda$-products across distinct intervals.

lem:6.4.summary: Hybrid second-moment lemma combining lem:6.2 and lem:6.3 — the variance bound powering thm:6.

edges.note: Chain construction directly yields the main bound.
Bipartite covering theorem is the probabilistic engine.

main_result.description: thm:1 — chains of $k$ consecutive large gaps, generalizing the single-gap bound from 1408.5110 and 1408.4505.

chain_construction.description: thm:5, thm:6, cor:2 and the lem:6.* lemmas execute the chain construction by combining the deterministic and random sieves over $k$ consecutive intervals with controlled variance.

deterministic_sieve_branch.description: thm:2 (interval sieve) + lem:3.1 ($B_0$ selection) — the Maynard 1408.5110 deterministic side, extended to chains.

random_covering_branch.description: thm:3 (prime sieve) + thm:4 (abstract bipartite covering) + lem:1.1 (second-moment Chebyshev) — the FGKT 1408.4505 probabilistic side, extended to chains.

L_function_zero_free_layer.description: lem:2.1 (Landau-Page), cor:1 (Siegel modulus extraction), lem:2.2 (Gallagher PNT). Standard analytic input controlling exceptional zeros.

FGKT:largegaps.title: Large gaps between consecutive prime numbers

Erdos:1948.title: Some problems on the distribution of prime numbers

Rankin:1938.title: On the difference of consecutive primes
